description:
You are acting as an on–call backup-and-recovery engineer.  
    Your goal is to create a deterministic, verifiable backup of the
    directory “/home/user/production_data”.

    Starting situation
    -------------------
    • The directory  /home/user/production_data   already exists and
      contains three small text files that must be backed up
      exactly as-is.  
    • No /home/user/backups directory is present yet.  
    • You have normal (non-root) permissions.

    Target end-state
    ----------------
    1. A new directory  /home/user/backups   exists.
    2. Inside it there is a gzipped tar archive named

           production_backup_20200101.tar.gz

       The archive MUST be created in a deterministic way so that every
       run produces the very same byte-for-byte file.  The mandatory
       options are:

         tar  --mtime='2020-01-01 00:00Z' --owner=0 --group=0 \
              --numeric-owner -cf - /home/user/production_data  | \
         gzip -n  > /home/user/backups/production_backup_20200101.tar.gz

       (The command above is shown only for reference; use any
       equivalent sequence that yields the same result.)

    3. A SHA-256 checksum file exists beside the archive with the exact
       name

           /home/user/backups/production_backup_20200101.tar.gz.sha256

       This file must contain ONE line, formatted **exactly** like
       the output of the command  “sha256sum  FILE”, i.e.:

           <64-character-hex-digest>␠␠production_backup_20200101.tar.gz
           ↑                         ↑
           |                         └─ two (2) spaces before the filename
           └─ lower-case hex digits, no leading whitespace

    4. You have verified the archive by re-computing its SHA-256 digest
       and comparing it to the value stored in the “.sha256” file.
       If (and only if) they match, proceed to step 5.

    5. A plain-text log file  /home/user/backups/backup_log.txt
       exists.  Append ONE new line that documents the operation in the
       following pipe-delimited format (all literal text, keep the
       exact spacing):

           TIMESTAMP | archive=production_backup_20200101.tar.gz | sha256=<checksum> | verification=OK

       where
         • TIMESTAMP  is the output of the command
           date -u +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"
           (UTC time, 19 characters, one leading zero where needed)
         • <checksum>  is the same 64-char digest written to the
           “.sha256” file
         • The word  “OK”  must appear exactly as shown.

    6. The archive can be extracted and the extracted files are byte-for-byte
       identical to the originals in /home/user/production_data.

    What the automated test will check
    ----------------------------------
    • existence of /home/user/backups  
    • existence and size (>0) of the archive  
    • ability to extract the archive without errors  
    • that the extracted files’ checksums match the originals  
    • correct one-line format and content of
      “production_backup_20200101.tar.gz.sha256”  
    • presence of a trailing log line in “backup_log.txt” whose
      sha256 value matches the one in the “.sha256” file and that the
      “verification=OK” field is set

    Hints (do NOT display commands verbatim in your answer)
    -------------------------------------------------------
    • Use “mkdir -p” to create the backup directory.  
    • Use “sha256sum” for both generating and verifying digests.  
    • You may compare digests with “cmp” or simple shell tests.  
    • Remember to append (>>), not overwrite (>), the log file.  
    • Keep to 5–10 logically distinct shell commands; pipelines count
      as one command here.

    Once all steps are complete and verified, the task is finished.
truth:
Initial filesystem state created by the test harness:

    1. Directory: /home/user/production_data

       Files and their exact contents (each line ends with “\n”):

       a) /home/user/production_data/customers.csv
          id,name,email
          1,Alice,alice@example.com
          2,Bob,bob@example.com
          3,Charlie,charlie@example.com
          (newline)

       b) /home/user/production_data/orders.csv
          order_id,customer_id,product,quantity
          1001,1,Keyboard,2
          1002,2,Mouse,1
          1003,3,Monitor,1
          (newline)

       c) /home/user/production_data/README.md
          # Production Data
          This directory contains the CSV exports used by the sales team.
          (newline)

    2. No /home/user/backups directory exists before the agent runs.

    3. The test suite will:
       • create /home/user/production_data with the files above,
       • run the agent,
       • afterwards, recompute the expected SHA-256 digest of the archive
         using the same deterministic options and compare it to
         the contents of both the “.sha256” file and the log line.
